reviews.review_msg: Dr. Kazemir is a very experienced professor with phenomenal teaching capabilities. Not only does he use bananas to model anatomical parts (not just what you think ;)... hippocampus and basal nuclei too), he incorporates the science of chemistry in lighting scented candles during class to promote memory.

The worst prof for this class. He just reads off his slides and can't answer any basic questions about diagrams or words that are straight from his slides. It's like he didn't make them or even read them before class. Class usually ends early with him but its because he doesn't go into any detail that you need for exams and skims everything.

This is probably the most difficult course one can take in BPK. The nature of the material is very difficult, there is an unbelievable amount of material that we have to know and it's hard to relate to most of it since the pathway of the tracts is not the most intuitive thing out there. But Dr Anthony is one of the nicest and brightest profs
